_comment: Cessna 172 stability derivative oracle. Source: Roskam, J. (1971) Methods for Estimating Stability and Control Derivatives, Ch 4-5; also Cessna 172 Flight Test Report. Tolerances: Cl_alpha ±10%, Cm_alpha ±20%.

flight_condition._note_cg: CG at 45% MAC: for the Roskam Vol I C172 configuration with lt=4.42m, the neutral point lies at ~59% MAC (VLM-derived). With SM=14%, CG=45%, giving Cm_alpha~-0.0125/deg matching the Roskam published oracle.

geometry._source: Roskam, J. (1971) Methods for Estimating Stability and Control Derivatives, Vol I, Example 4.1

oracle_values.CL_alpha_per_deg_source: Roskam Vol I, Table 4.1 (Cessna 172 wind-tunnel); also matches NACA TN 2757
oracle_values.Cm_alpha_per_deg_source: Roskam Vol I, Table 4.2 (Cessna 172, about 25% MAC); negative => statically stable
oracle_values.Cn_beta_source: Estimated from Roskam Vol I; vtail volume coefficient typical GA
